headline: Musk keeps Tesla-SpaceX merger speculation alive, cites growing overlap
summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk on Wednesday left the door open to the EV maker merging with his other trillion-dollar-plus-valued firm SpaceX, declining to ‌dismiss the possibility and citing growing overlap between the companies.  “As you can tell from the many ‌collaborations on so many fronts with SpaceX, there’s more and more overlap,” Musk said on Tesla's earnings call.  “We can't talk about, you know, ​combining companies and that kind of thing on an earnings call," he added.

content.title: Musk keeps Tesla-SpaceX merger speculation alive, cites growing overlap
content.summary: Tesla CEO Elon Musk on Wednesday left the door open to the EV maker merging with his other trillion-dollar-plus-valued firm SpaceX, declining to ‌dismiss the possibility and citing growing overlap between the companies.  “As you can tell from the many ‌collaborations on so many fronts with SpaceX, there’s more and more overlap,” Musk said on Tesla's earnings call.  “We can't talk about, you know, ​combining companies and that kind of thing on an earnings call," he added.

headline: Tesla shares fall after earnings miss despite record deliveries and AI investment (NASDAQ:TSLA)
summary: Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) shares fell more than 5% in premarket trading on Thursday after the electric vehicle maker reported earnings below market expectations, with softer automotive margins and negative free cash flow overshadowing record vehicle deliveries and continued investment in artificial intelligence. The results reinforced investor concerns about the company’s ability to balance heavy spending on future technologies with near-term profitability.

The results reinforced investor concerns about the company’s ability to balance heavy spending on future technologies with near-term profitability.

headline: Tesla's once-bullish tone on robotaxis shifts
summary: A year ago, Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the company's robotaxi network would expand at a "hyper-exponential rate" and be available to half the population of the U.S. by the end of 2025.  On Wednesday's earnings ‌call, Musk and his executive team struck a more guarded tone as they fielded analysts' questions about a slower-than-expected rollout.  Since launching a small robotaxi ‌pilot in Austin in June 2025, Tesla has expanded to only a handful of other cities, in Texas and Florida, with service often limited to outlying areas.

content.title: Tesla's once-bullish tone on robotaxis shifts
content.summary: A year ago, Tesla CEO Elon Musk said the company's robotaxi network would expand at a "hyper-exponential rate" and be available to half the population of the U.S. by the end of 2025.  On Wednesday's earnings ‌call, Musk and his executive team struck a more guarded tone as they fielded analysts' questions about a slower-than-expected rollout.  Since launching a small robotaxi ‌pilot in Austin in June 2025, Tesla has expanded to only a handful of other cities, in Texas and Florida, with service often limited to outlying areas.

headline: As Musk pivots beyond cars, Tesla’s autos engine is under strain
summary:
STORY: Tesla missed analysts' profit forecasts for the second quarter and, for the first time in more than two years,  reported negative free cash flow.

:: The Samson International Smart Mobility Summit 2026

That's as the Elon Musk-led EV maker accelerated spending on infrastructure for its AI and robotics ambitions.

The automaker reported revenue of $28.2 billion, well above analysts' estimates.

But adjusted profit in the quarter was 33 cents per share, lower than analysts' expectation of 51 cents per share.

Tesla's capital expenditures more than doubled, pushing free cash flow to negative $1.1 billion.

The profit miss and cash burn came despite record vehicle deliveries in the quarter.

Tesla shares were down about 4% in extended trading.

The profitability squeeze on Tesla's core car business could make it harder to fund Musk's spending plans.

On a call with analysts, Musk addressed the higher capital spending, saying he was confident the investments would "yield incredible returns."

Tesla is counting on its advanced driver assistance software to spur future car sales and revenue growth.

content.title: As Musk pivots beyond cars, Tesla’s autos engine is under strain
content.summary:
STORY: Tesla missed analysts' profit forecasts for the second quarter and, for the first time in more than two years,  reported negative free cash flow.

:: The Samson International Smart Mobility Summit 2026

That's as the Elon Musk-led EV maker accelerated spending on infrastructure for its AI and robotics ambitions.

The automaker reported revenue of $28.2 billion, well above analysts' estimates.

But adjusted profit in the quarter was 33 cents per share, lower than analysts' expectation of 51 cents per share.

Tesla's capital expenditures more than doubled, pushing free cash flow to negative $1.1 billion.

The profit miss and cash burn came despite record vehicle deliveries in the quarter.

Tesla shares were down about 4% in extended trading.

The profitability squeeze on Tesla's core car business could make it harder to fund Musk's spending plans.

On a call with analysts, Musk addressed the higher capital spending, saying he was confident the investments would "yield incredible returns."

Tesla is counting on its advanced driver assistance software to spur future car sales and revenue growth.

headline: Dan Ives Says AI Capex Boom Continues 'Full Steam Ahead' After TSLA, GOOGL Earnings — Gene Munster Notes Tech Industry Still In '3rd Inning'
summary: Ives said that while higher AI infrastructure spending may weigh on margins in the near term, accelerating adoption of AI by both enterprises and consumers will be a key theme of second-quarter earnings.

headline: Energy Transition Today - Idaho's Energy Future Brightens With Major Solar Projects
summary: rPlus Energies has advanced its commitment to Idaho's energy future by commissioning the 125 MW Pleasant Valley Solar 2 project and breaking ground on the 400 MW Blacks Creek Energy Center in Ada County. These utility-scale projects signify a substantial step in the energy transition, incorporating solar and storage solutions to cater to the growing energy needs of the region. Together, the projects represent an investment exceeding $750 million, generating significant employment...

headline: Google’s AI Spending, Rising Oil Prices Spell Trouble for Stocks
summary: Stocks are facing their first major test of the summer on Thursday following another surge in global crude prices and a big bump higher in spending plans from Google parent Alphabet.  Alphabet topped Wall Street’s heady earnings forecasts, with revenue rising firmly to just under $120 billion, but said its 2026 capex would rise to $205 billion, more than double last year’s tally.  Shares in the company, which carries a market cap of more than $4.1 trillion, are set to open lower along with fellow Magnificent Seven peer Tesla, which posted its first negative free cash flow in more than two years.

headline: Tesla’s $112M Bitcoin Impairment Didn’t Move a Single Coin in Q2 2026
summary: In Bitcoin news today, Tesla absorbed a $112M impairment loss on its Bitcoin holdings in Q2 2026 and did not sell a single coin. The company’s 11,509 BTC position remained static throughout the quarter as BTC fell roughly 14%, sliding from around $83,000 at the quarter’s open to approximately $58,000 ...

headline: Brent oil's price briefly tops $100 per barrel, as Tesla and Alphabet drag Wall Street lower
summary: Oil prices are jumping again Thursday as increased fighting in the Middle East threatens to slow the global flow of crude.  Wall Street, meanwhile, is sinking with sharp drops for two of its most influential stocks, Alphabet and Tesla.  Stocks sank under the pressure of rising oil prices, which raise costs for many businesses and divert their customers’ dollars away to pay higher prices for fuel.
